heading: Grants to States for access and visitation programs
label: 669b Grants to States for access and visitation programs
notes: Effective DateFor effective date of section, see section 395(a)–(c) of , set out as an Effective Date of 1996 Amendment note under .Pub. L. 104–193section 654 of this title
amendments: Effective DateFor effective date of section, see section 395(a)–(c) of , set out as an Effective Date of 1996 Amendment note under .Pub. L. 104–193section 654 of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The Administration for Children and Families shall make grants under this section to enable States to establish and administer programs to support and facilitate noncustodial parents’ access to and visitation of their children, by means of activities including mediation (both voluntary and mandatory), counseling, education, development of parenting plans, visitation enforcement (including monitoring, supervision and neutral drop-off and pickup), and development of guidelines for visitation and alternative custody arrangements.
90 percent of State expenditures during the fiscal year for activities described in subsection (a); or
the allotment of the State under subsection (c) for the fiscal year.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The allotment of a State for a fiscal year is the amount that bears the same ratio to $10,000,000 for grants under this section for the fiscal year as the number of children in the State living with only 1 biological parent bears to the total number of such children in all States.

children.heading: No supplantation of State expenditures for similar activities
children.label: d No supplantation of State expenditures for similar activities
children.text: A State to which a grant is made under this section may not use the grant to supplant expenditures by the State for activities specified in subsection (a), but shall use the grant to supplement such expenditures at a level at least equal to the level of such expenditures for fiscal year 1995.
may administer State programs funded with the grant, directly or through grants to or contracts with courts, local public agencies, or nonprofit private entities;
shall not be required to operate such programs on a statewide basis; and
shall monitor, evaluate, and report on such programs in accordance with regulations prescribed by the Secretary.
